completion_wording: DM1 V1 movement proof status: source-locked and runtime-supported from controlled key to queue predicates to dispatch-equivalent source chain to party tuple mutation to F0128 draw consumption. Direct F0380 entry/body BP remains blocked and is explicitly not claimed or required by this gate.

direct_f0380_decision.pass278: Staged BPM disable/re-enable reduced queue/index noise and captured controlled-key G0432 writes, party tuple mutation, and an F0128 draw BP hit after switching to code-only mode; the exact remaining blocker is no proven F0380 dequeue BP hit at 22F4:0699. This is not a runtime-hook claim.
direct_f0380_decision.pass289: Direct F0380 body breakpoint at pass278 address 22F4:0699 still does not hit; reproduce with pass278 route and breakpoints from its manifest/transcript. The dispatch-equivalent seam is GAMELOOP.C:215 -> COMMAND.C:F0380 source ordering, not a direct runtime BP claim.
direct_f0380_decision.pass293: No direct F0380 runtime proof. The source/map chain still points to public symbol 1BC1:0699 + load segment 0733 = 22F4:0699, and pass293 could set that BP in a broad candidate window, but the transcript contains no execution stop line at 22F4:0699. The broad window was additionally noisy on G0433 first-index writes and did not improve pass278. Next narrow step: run with only BP 22F4:0699 plus queue tuple watches, then if still silent disassemble/MEMDUMP live COMMAND_TEXT around 22F4:0680..06F0 and place BPs on post-prologue/dequeue instruction offsets rather than the public entry only.

proof_chain.source: COMMAND.C:F0361 writes G0432/G0434/G2153; COMMAND.C:F0380 reads/dequeues G0432/G0433

proof_chain.runtime_support: pass289 dispatch_equivalent_proven=true via pass278 runtime predicates
proof_chain.source: GAMELOOP.C:F0002 calls F0361 then F0380; COMMAND.C:F0380 dispatches F0365/F0366

proof_chain.runtime_support: pass278 party_tuple_mutation_seen=true with CPU/MEMDUMP after stops
proof_chain.source: MOVESENS.C:F0267 writes G0306/G0307 and uses G0308 direction path
GAMELOOP.C:88-90 passes G0308/G0306/G0307; DUNVIEW.C:F0128 receives P0183/P0184/P0185 and draws viewport

source_checks.claim: PC-34 controlled movement keys map to turn/move commands before enqueue.
F0361 locks the queue, selects the next slot, writes G0432, advances G0434, increments the guarded count, unlocks, then processes pending click replay.
The active game loop scans keys with F0361 and then invokes F0380, creating the accepted caller seam when direct F0380 entry BP misses.
F0380 source semantics dequeue G0432/G0433, apply the disabled-movement gate, advance/wrap the queue, replay pending click, then dispatch turn/move commands.
Successful movement writes the party tuple G0306/G0307 and calls direction/draw paths for movement consequences.
The main redraw passes the current party tuple G0308/G0306/G0307 into F0128.
F0128 receives direction/mapX/mapY arguments and finishes by drawing the dungeon viewport.
